1. Chase‘s profits & years of iterations/„improvements“ still has an app that is unprofessional/incomplete/impractical/frustrating in ui/ux.
The “Secure message Center” to communicate with bank is so limited, impractical for the real world: it warns you of “ You can use any special characters except: } > ₹ { " " ' ' ÷ × <” but who can write without those characters?! Not to mention, it wastes tons of time to figure out why mere ? and comma (,) and period (.) does not allow you to send a message! How bloody simple it pure text, why would a system be programmed on purpose to not accept basic characters?! What is IT/management thinking/approving?! 

2. ALERTS: links to Alerts History but it only lists accounts and there is no indication in which the new unchecked alert is, so that one has to waste time clicking in each account to see where new alert is?! Do software engineers live in the real world?! Be realistic, use your tools before you finish coding!!

3. Chase & other corporations should be more user-oriented, not so focused on their image & ratings & how much they satisfy us after every bloody call/eMail/correspondence etc. 

Engineers/programmers should be banned from designing apps, they are unrealistic.

Chase surely can afford better programming, better more realistic featured-app!

title: Chase Review
review: Have been a long time Chase customer. It seems that COVID/Coronavirus was a good excuse for many to mishandle many things. The supply chain must have fluctuated during it, and subsequently NOT consequently, Chase deleted my entire account that has a full history of my transaction and also accrued points without proper explanation or informing the actual account owner, who obviously is myself. It effectively blocked any financial transactions that were needed on my end, backfiring and causing mayhem and distress on their end. Full of error and mishandling. Needs a full review and correction. Update: They even started lying about not having my contact information registered. A futile and rather pathetic attempt to cover up their failure and wrongdoings and also evade consequences. Either extremely incompetent or fraudulent. Do not engage. Their branches were filled with employees covered with tattoos and artificial lashes and nails with questionable hygiene. Overall, display of extremely low behavior considering the size of its company. It even try to frame it as my wrongdoing, that their poor behavior is indeed in response to my lack of commitment to their misbehavior. All they are trying to do is find someone and somewhere to dump their responsibility and held accountable for their misdeed. NOT recommended.

title: Poor MFA Support
review:
Chase is one of the largest banks in the world, yet its app-to-website MFA experience feels like it was designed by teams that have never spoken to each other—or tested their own product.

The core issue is multi-factor authentication when using the website in parallel with the mobile app. In theory, the app is supposed to facilitate secure login. In practice, it actively sabotages it. Authentication prompts arrive late, don’t arrive at all, or arrive after the web session has already timed out. Sometimes approving a login in the app inexplicably logs you out of the website. Other times it triggers a loop where the site repeatedly asks for verification while the app insists everything is already approved.

title: Horrible mortgage process
review: This review is about getting a mortgage with Chase. It has been nothing but a complete disorganized nightmare with them. First off, after the loan contingency date, they were requesting documents at the LAST MINUTE. They could have requested them weeks beforehand. I had to make phone calls, talk to managers and demand to get these documents in order and send to Chase. It was time consuming. Second, Chase got my insurance information wrong for the house. Instead of catching it 2 weeks beforehand, they tell me THE DATE OF CLOSING that information is wrong on the insurance policy. ARE YOU KIDDING ME?!?!?! That could have been fixed weeks ago, and they’re asking me LAST MINUTE. What is wrong with these people? I’ve been panicking and hassling to get things squared away for them. I seriously feel like I’m doing their jobs for them, while they’re the ones screwing up. Absolutely ridiculous especially coming from such an institution. I expected better! Don’t go with Chase or else you’ll be chasing documents and policies like a chicken with your head cut off! I’m at risk of losing my $4,300 deposit on this house because of this! The house was supposed to close today and Chase can’t get things in order and have it ready to go!

title: App barely passes standards for a usable experience in 2025
review:
I’ve begrudgingly used this app for years despite how outdated a lot of its features are. Today I went to edit my travel profile and add a new rewards program to my profile, and even though the title of the page says update profile in small type under my name it says that I can only do that from the desktop website. In what world is it acceptable to pay this much for a credit card and have an app where I can’t manage my profile via the app or my phone. I don’t have a desktop computer anymore. It’s 2025. 

The embedded web views are hard enough to use and navigate whenever I’m booking travel I end up having to start over multiple times by accidentally pressing the wrong button. 

Every time I use this app, I get frustrated by something that has not been a good experience as a customer.
